author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Added generic ufunc methods in new ferray-ufunc/src/ufunc_methods.rs module:

- reduce_axis(arr, axis, identity, op) -> Array<T, IxDyn> — collapse axis by folding with any Fn(T, T) -> T, seeded by identity
- accumulate_axis(arr, axis, op) -> Array<T, D> — running fold along axis, output shape matches input
- outer(a, b, op) -> Array<T, IxDyn> — 1D x 1D outer product with any binary op
- at(arr, indices, values, op) -> in-place unbuffered scatter-reduce (matches NumPy's duplicate-index semantics — duplicate indices are applied in order, not just the last write)

The existing hand-written specializations (add_reduce, multiply_outer) now delegate to the new generic functions, so there's no duplicated traversal code. Callers who want subtract.reduce or power.outer or max.accumulate can now build them in one line:

    reduce_axis(&a, 0, f64::NEG_INFINITY, f64::max)   // np.max
    accumulate_axis(&a, 0, |acc, x| acc - x)           // subtract.accumulate
    outer(&a, &b, |x, y| x.powf(y))                    // power.outer

18 new unit tests cover: 1D/2D reductions (add/multiply/max) along both axes, running add/multiply/subtract, multiply/add/power outer products, unbuffered scatter-reduce with duplicate indices, and edge cases (axis out of bounds, length mismatch, index out of range).

236 lib tests + 50/19/53 integration pass. Clippy clean. Full workspace builds.

Reduceat and the truly-general N-D at() are deferred; they need either a ufunc object (#376) or a more elaborate indexing API and are lower priority than the common cases covered here.
